Q1.question: What was the company's total annual revenue for the fiscal year, and what was the year-over-year growth rate?
Q1.answer: Total annual revenue was $2,147M with year-over-year growth of 14.3%.

components.label: total_revenue
keywords: two billion one hundred and forty-seven

Q1.distractors_note: Quarterly figures ($498M, $523M, $547M, $579M), prior-year $1,879M, recurring revenue $1,612M, run-rate $2.4B

Q2.question: Who were the three new C-suite executives appointed during the fiscal year, what role does each hold, and which strategic area is each responsible for?
Q2.answer: Dr. Elena Vasquez as CTO (technology and product innovation), Marcus Chen as CFO (finance, strategy, and investor relations), Sarah Okonkwo as COO (global operations and supply chain).
Q2.distractors_note: Dr. Raymond Liu (SVP, not C-suite), Jennifer Walsh (board member, not executive), David Hartley (existing CEO)

Q3.question: What is the company's total R&D budget for the year and how much funding was allocated to each of the three incubation programmes?
Q3.answer: Total R&D: $412M. Incubation programmes: autonomous operations $45M, privacy-preserving ML $28M, edge intelligence $37M.
Q3.distractors_note: Prior-year R&D $348M, engineering CapEx $89M, partner co-investment $17M, total incubation $110M

Q4.question: What was the overall production platform uptime achieved this year and what new security certifications were obtained during the fiscal year?
Q4.answer: Overall platform uptime was 99.98%. New certifications: ISO 27001:2022 upgrade and FedRAMP Moderate Authorization.
Q4.distractors_note: Analytics service 99.95%, prior-year 99.93%, SLA target 99.95%, aspirational 99.99%, pursuing FedRAMP High (not achieved), ISO 27701 (planned), PCI DSS (pre-existing)

Q5.question: How has the company's overall customer retention rate changed over the past three years, and what specific programmes were credited with driving the improvement?
Q5.answer: Retention improved from 87.2% three years ago to 93.7% this year. Key programmes: dedicated success architects, proactive health scoring, quarterly business reviews, and the technology alliance programme's integration connectors.
Q5.distractors_note: Enterprise retention 97.2% (segment only), SMB 88.3%, CSAT 87% (different metric), trial conversion 34%

Q6.question: How much did the company's sustainability initiatives save in total operational costs this year, and what were the three largest contributing programmes with their individual savings?
Q6.answer: Total: $31M. Three largest: data-centre energy optimisation ($18.4M), fleet electrification ($7.8M), waste reduction & circular procurement ($4.8M).
Q6.distractors_note: Prior-year savings $19M, projected future $42M, Dallas retrofit alone $11M, procurement savings $37M (different category)

Q7.question: How many net new positions is the company planning to add next fiscal year, and how will the hiring be distributed across the major functional areas?
Q7.answer: 1,800 net new positions: ~700 in engineering & product, ~350 in sales & customer success, ~750 in operations & G&A.
Q7.distractors_note: Current headcount 12,347, prior year 10,912, gross hiring 2,100 (not net), contractors 3,200, AI team target 340 (subset)
